phrasal_verbs.meaning: find or see someone or something when you did not expect to
meet someone or find something by chance
become cooler, or make something cooler
reduce the amount of something, especially money that you spend
become weaker or less common and then disappear completely
remove something from under the ground by digging; dig holes in an area of land; find information by searching carefully
if something dries up or is dried up, all the water comes out of it; stop being available
use or finish something; manage to deal with a difficult situation or stay alive until it is over; finish dealing with some work, a subject, etc
destroy living things so that most or all of them are dead
if you put something down to a particular reason, you think it has happened for that reason
appear or be produced suddenly and quickly
keep a lot of something so that you can use it later; do something that will cause problems in the future

get rid of something that you no longer want, for example by putting it in a dustbin; force someone to leave a place or group; if someone in authority throws out a plan, proposal, etc they refuse to accept it
produce something new or unexpected; cause something such as dust or water to rise into the air

odds: the odds are in favour of/against

idioms.meaning: an event such as a flood, lightning or an earthquake that is produced by natural forces, and that people have no control over
used for saying that something always happens or someone always does something despite bad weather or difficult conditions
having a long period of difficulty in doing work and earning money
be chosen to do something unpleasant
allow something to develop without trying to influence it
used for emphasising that there is no reason or explanation for something happening in a way that is sudden and unexpected, and does not seem connected with anything that happened before
used when you have said that you have had good luck in order to prevent bad luck from happening to you
used for saying people have limited control over what life brings them
used when you have said that you have had good luck in order to prevent bad luck from happening to you

A.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: The woman claimed to be able to ……………………. the future, but I knew she was only guessing.
In a moment of madness, I decided to ……………………… everything on the turn of one card.
It seems that one of the genes has ………………………… , causing the frog to be blue.
Following the investigation, the accident was ……………………… to chance.
When the glass fell off the table on its own, it really ……………………… me.
A casino's profits might ……………………… from month to month, but you can be sure they will always make money.
Police have yet to ……………………… what caused the building to collapse.
The first I knew of the earthquake was when the furniture started to ……………………………… .
Is there a winning roulette system that can help you …………………………… the right number?
As the river gets closer to the sea, it begins to ……………………… across the flat lowlands.

B.title: Choose the correct answer
B.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C or D) to complete each sentence.

questions.sentence: What's the ………………… number of accidents which happen in the home each year?
I always take my lucky ………………… with me into an exam.
Did you plan to meet up with Emma in London, or was it just a ………………… ?
Johnnie broke the vase, but I'm sure it wasn't ………………… .
We can only ………………… as to the causes of the disaster.
The ………………… of one winning the lottery is hardly worth even considering.
I didn't know my guess was going to be right – it was just ………………… .
Do you believe in that old ………………… about not walking under a ladder?

C.title: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes
C.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word from the box.

questions.sentence: Have you ever done something completely …………………, like suddenly deciding to quit your job?
This government doesn't seem to have a plan and is just reacting to events in a ………………… manner.
Having an accident in the first race was a real ………………… to Johnson's ambitions of becoming world champion.
We should get there by midnight, as long as there are no ………………… along the way.

D.title: Correct the wrong words in bold
D.instructions: If the word or phrase in bold is correct, put a tick. If it is incorrect, replace it with a word or phrase in bold from another sentence.

questions.sentence: The earthquake was a real **resource** and destroyed most of the buildings in the city.
They are going to **harvest** the local people because the volcano might erupt.
We need to find alternatives to **catastrophe** before we destroy our planet.
There's so much **fossil fuels** in England during the summer.
The panda is threatened by the destruction of its **greenery**.
They were planning to **evacuate** the grapes the next day and nobody could have predicted the storm.
Some animals seem to know by **species** when bad weather is on the way.
The oil fields are a vast natural **habitat** that belongs to all the people, not just a few businessmen.
The sparrow is becoming increasingly **scarce** in this area.
Scientists claim to have identified a new **instinct** of monkey.

E.title: Circle the correct word or phrase
E.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: It seems that **agriculture / crop** first developed in the Middle East.
Coffee is a very important **agriculture / crop** in Brazil.
The **drought / famine** was caused by war in the region.
If the **drought / famine** continues, the government is planning to introduce a ban on washing cars.
You have to stop and get out of your car to really **appreciate / exploit** the natural beauty of the area.
Why do you think that we don't have the right to **appreciate / exploit** nature to make money?
Some scientists still dispute that **global warming / natural disaster** is caused by human activity.
Over half a million people were forced to leave their homes as a result of the recent **global warming / natural disaster**.
It's been raining for days and there's a real danger of **floods / hurricanes** if it doesn't stop soon.
This area is frequently hit by **floods / hurricanes** that start at sea and then move inland.

F.title: Write one word in each gap (phrasal verbs)
F.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text about global warming.

questions.sentence: The majority of scientists put it (1) …………… to human activity.
The solution, they claim, is to cut back (2) …………… our consumption of fossil fuels.
The Earth heats (3) …………… and cools (4) …………… naturally.
We should just continue to get (5) …………… the fossil fuels that are available.
Many species will die (6) …………… as once fertile areas turn to desert.
Rivers will dry (7) …………… in other areas.
Killing (8) …………… animal and plant life that rely on them.

G.title: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs
G.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the phrasal verbs in the box.

questions.sentence: We were walking through the woods when we ……………………………………… a trap set by hunters.
It's a huge risk, and we can't afford to …………………………………………… or everything will go wrong.
Don't just ……………………………………… those plastic bottles – we can recycle them.
Isn't it amazing how squirrels ……………………………………… nuts to make sure they have enough to eat in the winter?
The new law has ……………………………………… a number of problems for farmers that weren't expected.
As I was flicking through the magazine, I ……………………………………… an article about the local nature reserve.
According to the evidence that has been ……………………………………… , people have been farming this area for over a 1,000 years.
Organisations concerned about the environment seem to have ……………………………………… everywhere these days.

H.title: Phrasal verbs with 'off' meaning 'remove'
H.instructions: Put the following phrasal verbs into the sentences in the correct form and tick the sentences where the phrasal verb means something like 'remove'.

questions.sentence: After what Maria said, I think we should ...... her ...... the guest list.
Calvin ...... the chocolate ...... his mouth with the back of his hand.
Do you think Dad will ever ...... his beard ...... ?
There's a funny smell, like something's ...... in the fridge.
Aren't you going to ...... the head ...... the fish before you serve it?
I'm so tired after work that I often just ...... in front of the TV.
We're going to ...... early in the morning, so try to get some sleep.
You need to ...... the paper ...... the top of the ice cream before you eat it.

I.title: Write one preposition in each gap
I.instructions: Write one preposition in each gap.

questions.sentence: It's a million to one against you being struck ………………….. lightning.
We got caught …………………… the rain so we were completely soaked when we got home.
Many people only manage to give up gambling ………………… dint of a lot of hard work.
A sudden gust …………………… wind blew the letter out of my hand.
I can't believe you bet your car …………………… the toss of a coin!
I'd love to be a cat and spend all day lying …………………… the sun.
I believe we're …………………… luck this time and we won't have lost our money.
You're either lucky …………………… nature or you have to work hard all your life.

J.title: Write one word in each gap (text)
J.instructions: Write one word in each gap to complete the text about earthquake prediction.

questions.sentence: If you were to hazard a (1) …………… at when and where the next major earthquake will strike.
There's (2) …………… chance that you'd be almost as accurate as the experts.
Considering the threat posed (3) …………… human civilization.
Even with vast resources at (4) …………… disposal.
The best that scientists can do is say that (5) …………… odds are that an earthquake will strike where one occurred before.
One reason the chances (6) …………… making an accurate prediction are so low.
Is the nature (7) …………… the forces involved.
Scientists need to decide whether to put lives at (8) …………… by not issuing a warning.
Or constantly issue warnings about the threat (9) …………… an earthquake that may be ignored.
The odds seem to be (10) …………… us solving this difficult problem any time soon.

K.title: Write one word in each gap (collocations)
K.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: I think Jay plays poker, but I couldn't ………………………………… for certain.
If one species survives at the expense of another, then I think that's just ………………………………… selection.
I'll just call Jimmy to ………………………………… certain he can look after the dogs.
The Minister gave the ………………………………… light for the bypass to go ahead.
You were a bit hard on him for crashing your car – give him a ………………………………… chance!
People are ………………………………… their luck a bit too far when they live on the slopes of a volcano.
Global warming will ………………………………… a great threat to mankind in years to come.
Just at the moment we had to go out, it started to ………………………………… with rain.

L.title: One word for three sentences
L.instructions: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

questions.sentence:
Have you ever ………………… yourself wondering what it would be like to be someone else?
I'm surprised you ………………… the test so difficult after all that revision.
After wandering in the forest for an hour, Gale finally ……………… her way out.

The problem of conservation is essentially economic in ………………… .
It's only human ………………… to want to acquire more wealth.
I'm quite a calm person by ………………… , but things like this make me really angry.

Let's go through things again; I don't want to leave anything to ………………… .
Excuse me, but are you Mr Whitehouse, by any ………………… ?
I mean it – this is your last ………………… and next time you make a mistake, you're out.

I haven't been at work all week because I've been a bit under the ……………… .
According to the ………………… forecast, we're in for thunderstorms.
We've had some really awful ………………… lately, haven't we?

I was wondering how to get home when, as ………………… would have it, Ben drove past in his car.
The assistant said that I was in ……………… and that they only had one pair left.
You've won by betting on red the last three times, but don't push your ………………… !

It so ……………… that I've already decided to do that, anyway.
As it ……………… , we can cancel the demonstration because the council have changed their mind.
When a freak storm ……………… , we are not always given any warning of it.

I have to admit to feeling a ……………… amount of fear as I stepped into the lions' cage.
The press are ……………… to find out about this, and then it'll be all over the papers.
A ……………… Mr Pratt rang this afternoon about your donation to the WWF.

I ……………… you must be right about the weather – it feels hot!
Don't ask me to ……………… at her age as I'd be miles out.
You'd never ……………… they were married from the way they act in public, would you?

M.title: Complete idioms
M.instructions: Complete each dialogue using the appropriate form of an idiom in the box.

questions.sentence: 'I feel sorry for Mrs Shepherd.' 'Yes, she does seem to have been rather ………………… lately, doesn't she?'
'I'm sure Carol will get over the divorce eventually.' 'Yes, I think we should just ………………… and see what happens.'
'I couldn't believe it when I saw him.' 'It must have been a real shock when he turned up ………………… after all these years.'
'Does Dave still go to the football every week?' 'Oh, yes. You know him. He's there ………………… .'
'How come the company chose you to go to New York?' 'I don't know. It was just …………………, I guess.'
'Why wasn't there any warning before the floods?' 'Well, it's not easy to predict that kind of …………………, is it?'
'I don't understand Harry's decision.' 'Me neither. There seems to be ………………… to it.'
'Who's babysitting tomorrow night?' 'Well, I …………………, so I'm staying in while the others go out.'
'I'm so looking forward to the wedding this weekend.' 'The weather should be good, ………………… .'
'I love your garden!' 'Thanks. Yes, I suppose I've always ………………… .'

N.title: Word formation (text)
N.instructions: Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the space.

questions.sentence: The World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) has been involved in (1) ………………… (CONSERVE) since its foundation in 1961.
Its (2) ………………… (INTEND) has always been to protect wildlife.
To protect (3) ………………… (WILD) against the effects of human activity.
Working very closely with both expert (4) ………………… (ECOLOGY) and ordinary local people.
The organisation aims to help those animals (5) ………………… (THREAT) by the destruction of habitats.
Economic growth and tourism force some species to the brink of (6) ………………… (EXTINCT).
(7) ………………… (FORTUNE), the WWF work hard to ensure that animals such as the giant panda will survive.
The giant panda will (8) ………………… (PROBABLE) survive for a few more years yet.
Refusing to accept that the problems are (9) ………………… (MOUNT).
The WWF works tirelessly on a (10) ………………… (SEEM) never-ending list of projects.

O.title: Negative prefixes
O.instructions: The prefix il-, as in illogical, is often used to make a positive word negative. Which of the words in bold in the following sentences are negative forms of positive words? Write YES or NO.

questions.sentence: Doctors' handwriting is usually **illegible**.
There's a beautiful **illustration** on page six.
People who are **illiterate** may need some help filling in forms.
She had an **illustrious** career as a local politician.
It's a totally **illiberal** law that's just going to restrict our freedoms further.
Several senior politicians have resigned over the **illegality** of the war.
**Illegitimate** children were given very little social recognition in the past.
The magician didn't really cut his assistant in two – it was only an **illusion**.
The lights **illuminated** the building beautifully at night.

P.title: The suffix -y
P.instructions: The suffix -y, as in risky, can be added to some nouns and verbs to form adjectives. Tick the words which can have -y added to them to make adjectives. You may need to double a final letter or remove a final -e.
P.answer: brainy, bumpy, curly, flavoury, guilty, hairy, hasty, juicy, lucky, lumpy, meaty, milky, scary, shiny, sporty, spotty, tasty, wasty, watery, wealthy
